shell.hero_title: Ordicab workspace - select your domain to get started.
shell.hero_summary: Schemas, stores, and the design-system baseline are ready so later stories can add behavior without rewiring core contracts.
shell.teaser_card_local_body: Point Ordicab to your usual folder. Nothing to move, nothing to import.
domain.onboarding_summary_select: Choose the root folder for your dossiers. Ordicab adds its hidden workspace there.
domain.setup_summary_onboarding: Select the folder that holds your client dossiers. Ordicab installs quietly, without touching your files.
domain.setup_step_choose_body: Select the folder with your dossiers.
domain.setup_step_resume_body: Open the dashboard and switch later if needed.
domain.setup_hint: Ordicab never moves or deletes your files.
legal.eula_summary: Please read and accept these terms before continuing to use Ordicab.
onboarding.beta_warning_title: Experimental version — Do not use in production
onboarding.beta_warning_body1_prefix: This software is a beta version for testing purposes only.
onboarding.beta_warning_body1_strong: Do not use it with real work data or client dossiers.
onboarding.beta_warning_body1_suffix: Bugs, data loss or unexpected behaviour may occur.
onboarding.beta_warning_body2_prefix: Use of artificial intelligence features (Claude Cowork, etc.) may involve transmitting data to third-party services.
onboarding.beta_warning_body2_strong: The user is solely responsible for GDPR compliance
onboarding.beta_warning_body2_suffix: particularly regarding the processing of personal data via AI tools and subscriptions.
onboarding.hero_title: Your client dossiers organised for AI, starting today.
onboarding.hero_summary: Tell Ordicab where your dossiers are. It structures your information so Claude Cowork answers correctly, from the very first question.
dashboard.section_summary_ready: Your domain is connected. You can continue in dashboard mode or switch to another domain from settings.
dashboard.settings_summary: Need to switch workspace? Use Change Domain. Ordicab never deletes files when changing domain.
dashboard.change_domain_confirm_body: Changing domain updates the active workspace only. Your existing files are not deleted or moved.
dossiers.section_summary: Register domain subfolders as active dossiers. Ordicab writes metadata only inside each dossier's hidden .ordicab folder.
dossiers.notice_unregistered: {{name}} has been removed from the dashboard.
dossiers.picker_summary: Only direct subfolders of the active domain can be registered. Ordicab never touches your existing documents.
dossiers.picker_search_placeholder: Type a dossier name or path
dossiers.picker_empty: Every eligible subfolder is already registered.
dossiers.empty_body: Start by registering one of your domain subfolders. Ordicab will create hidden metadata only and leave the rest untouched.
dossiers.filtered_empty_body: Try another dossier name or adjust the current status and sort filters.
dossiers.detail_empty_body: Choose a card to review and update its status, type, and upcoming work cues.
dossiers.detail_information_placeholder: Dossier summary, current status, useful context, next steps...
dossiers.detail_information_empty: No ongoing information for this dossier yet.
dossiers.ai_export_description: Generates an export folder containing dossier data, templates, and AI instructions. Open it with Claude Code or a compatible AI tool to create new documents, then import them back using the Import tab.
dossiers.ai_export_step1: Enable anonymization if you want to mask sensitive data before sharing the folder.
dossiers.ai_export_step2: Click 'Export for AI' and choose a destination folder.
dossiers.ai_export_step3: Open the exported AI folder with Claude Code: run claude inside the AI directory.
dossiers.ai_export_step4: Ask the AI to create documents — it writes them directly at the root of the AI folder.
dossiers.ai_export_step5: Come back to the Import tab to import the created files.
dossiers.ai_import_description: Select the exported AI folder to import the files created by the AI. Pseudonyms are replaced with real dossier data, and Ordicab macros ({{ }}) are resolved automatically. Imported files are indexed in your dossier.
dossiers.ai_export_missing_extraction: {{count}} document(s) still need text extraction before export.
contacts.emptyState: No contact yet. Add the first one ->
contacts.form.requiredHint: Display name is required. Role is recommended when known.
contacts.form.title: Title
contacts.form.role_duplicate_warning: A contact with this role already exists in this dossier.
contacts.form.managedFieldsHint: Additional fields proposed here come from the entity settings.
contacts.form.roleManagedFieldsHint: Additional fields proposed for the role "{{role}}".
contacts.form.noManagedFieldsForRole: No additional field is configured for this role.
contacts.form.hiddenManagedFields: Saved values kept outside the active selection
contacts.form.information_placeholder: Relationship to the dossier, ongoing actions, useful context...
entity.form.professionChangeWarning: Changing the activity type will recalculate the default contact roles and managed fields. Those lists will therefore be updated.
entity.section_summary: Save your firm details once so Ordicab can reuse them in templates and CLAUDE.md workflows.
entity.form.title: Title
entity.form.contactRoleManagedFieldsHint: Choose which additional fields should be proposed in dossiers and template macros for each business role.
entity.form.name: Name
entity.emptyHint: Start by adding your firm details once.
delegated.reference.title: AI Reference
delegated.reference.subtitle: All operations you can ask Claude to perform
delegated.offline.title: Offline workflow with Claude Code
delegated.offline.subtitle: To create or analyze documents with direct access to dossier files, use the AI export workflow.
delegated.offline.step1: In the dossier detail, go to the AI Export tab and click 'Export for AI'.
delegated.offline.step2: Open the exported folder in a terminal and run claude to start a Claude Code session.
delegated.offline.step3: Ask Claude to read the dossier data and create documents at the root of the AI folder.
delegated.offline.step4: Back in Ordicab, open the AI Import tab and import the created files — Ordicab macros and pseudonyms are resolved automatically.
delegated.reference.noDomain: Configure a domain to see context-aware prompts.
delegated.reference.operations.dossierBu: Create a complete dossier by describing the client, contacts, and key dates in plain language.

Set up dossier '{{dossierName}}' with the following information:
[paste all details - client name, contact roles, key dates, any references]
delegated.reference.operations.contactAd: Add one or more contacts to a dossier, or update existing contact details.

In dossier '{{dossierName}}', add or update the following contacts:
[paste contact details - name, role, email, phone]
delegated.reference.operations.keyDateEx: Extract key dates from a document and save them to a dossier.

In dossier '{{dossierName}}', extract and save all key dates from this text:
[paste the document excerpt here]
delegated.reference.operations.keyRefere: Add a procedural or administrative reference to a dossier.

In dossier '{{dossierName}}', add the following key reference:
[e.g., RG n°: 24/01234, or Ref: ABC-2024-001]
delegated.reference.operations.entitySet: Update the firm or practitioner profile with new details.

Update the firm entity profile for '{{entityName}}' with:
[paste updated details - SIRET, address, contact info]
delegated.reference.operations.documentT: Add metadata tags to documents in a dossier's document list.

In dossier '{{dossierName}}', tag the following documents with their type and date:
[paste document names and their document types]
delegated.reference.operations.documentA: Update the description and tags of one or more documents in a dossier.

In dossier '{{dossierName}}', update the annotations for the following documents:
[for each document: file name, new description, tags to add or remove]
delegated.reference.operations.templateA: Add a new document template or update an existing one for the firm.

For firm '{{entityName}}', add or update the following document template:
[template name, content with {{tags}}, optional description]
delegated.reference.operations.documentG: Render a template with dossier data and save the result as a file in the dossier folder.

In dossier '{{dossierName}}', generate a document using the following template:
[template name or id — Claude will render it with the dossier data and save it as a .txt file]
delegated.prompts.contacts:
In dossier '{{dossierName}}', add the following contacts:
[paste contact details here]
delegated.prompts.keyDates:
In dossier '{{dossierName}}', extract and add the following key dates:
[paste text with dates here]
delegated.prompts.keyReferences:
In dossier '{{dossierName}}', add the following key references:
[paste reference here]
delegated.prompts.entity:
Update the firm entity profile with:
[paste entity details here]
delegated.prompts.dossierSetup: Set up dossier '{{dossierName}}' with: status '[status]', type '[type]'
ordicab.sync.validation_failed: Claude wrote data that could not be parsed. Please check the file and try again.
documents.semantic_search_description: Find passages by meaning, not just keywords. Results rely on on-device embeddings of previously extracted documents.
documents.semantic_search_no_results: No matching passages. Try a different query or make sure documents have been extracted.
documents.no_results: No documents match the current filters
documents.metadata_description_placehold: Short note to identify this file quickly
documents.metadata_save_error: Unable to save document metadata right now.
documents.metadata_empty_hint: Add a short description or tags to make this file easier to spot.
documents.preview_empty_body: Select a document preview to inspect it here without leaving the dossier detail view.
documents.preview_error_body: Ordicab could not prepare this preview right now.
documents.preview_docx_invalid: This Word document preview could not be displayed. The file appears incomplete or invalid.
documents.extract_all_dialog_confirm_bod: This will extract text from {{count}} document(s). Depending on file sizes, this may take several minutes.
documents.extract_all_dialog_aborted_bod: Extraction stopped — {{succeeded}} succeeded, {{failed}} failed out of {{attempted}} processed.
documents.extraction_error_body: Ordicab could not extract readable text from this document right now.
documents.preview_text_empty: This document did not contain readable text content.
documents.preview_email_empty: This email did not contain readable text content.
documents.preview_pdf_error: Ordicab could not render this PDF preview.
dossiers.key_dates_empty: No key date yet. Add the next important deadline here.
dossiers.key_dates_information_placehold: Context, stakes, current progress, expected action...
dossiers.key_dates_form_hint: Associate a date with a key event in this dossier.
dossiers.key_dates_filter_search_placeho: Filter by label, date or note…
dossiers.key_dates_no_results: No key date matches your search.
dossiers.key_references_empty: No key reference yet. Add the identifiers you need most.
dossiers.key_references_form_hint: Associate an identifier or reference number with this dossier.
dossiers.unregister_confirm_body: Only Ordicab metadata inside .ordicab will be removed. Your documents stay exactly where they are.
settings.section_subtitle: Manage your workspace, firm profile and integrations.
nav.coming_soon_body: This section is not yet available.
templates.emptyState: Build a reusable library of document templates for future dossier generation.
templates.createChoice.title: Choose template type
templates.createChoice.description: Start with either an in-app rich text template or a Word document that stays the formatting source of truth.
templates.createChoice.textDescription: Write and edit the template directly in Ordicab.
templates.createChoice.docxDescription: Import a Word document and keep its formatting as the primary source.
templates.editor.createDocxDescription: Name the template first, then import the Word source.
templates.editor.description: Build reusable templates with rich text formatting and atomic macro chips.
templates.editor.name: Name
templates.editor.docxImportHint: Select a Word document — you can enter the name before or after.
templates.editor.createDocxHint: This flow creates a Word-backed template.
templates.editor.createDocxBody: Select a Word document to use as the source for this template. Word stays in full control of formatting, fonts, headers, and footers. Add Ordicab macros directly inside Word using the {{macro}} syntax.
templates.editor.docxPickedBody: Word document selected. Enter a name above then click Save to create the template.
templates.editor.docxSourceHint: This template uses a Word document as its source. Edit the app-managed companion text here, then place the placeholder below in the Word file where that text should appear.
templates.editor.docxPlaceholderHint: Place {{app.content}} in the .docx source to inject this app-managed text.
templates.editor.removeDocxConfirm: Remove the Word source? This template will return to text-only mode.
templates.editor.docxPreviewHint: Content extracted from Word — read-only. Open in Word to edit formatting and add macros.
templates.editor.docxEditHint: Content extracted from Word. You can edit it and insert macros — open in Word to adjust formatting.
templates.editor.richTextHint: Formatting and macros are preserved when you save.
templates.editor.docxFormattingHint: Word formatting stays in the .docx source; this editor injects text and macros into the placeholder only.
templates.editor.duplicateName: A template with this name already exists.
templates.tagPanel.title: Insert Macro
templates.tagPanel.helperText: Click any macro to insert it at the current cursor position.
templates.tagPanel.contactRoleHint: Choose a contact role to generate the matching macros.
templates.tagPanel.keyLabelHint: Type the label of your key date or reference to generate the matching macro.
templates.macros.title: Macros
templates.macros.helperText: Click any macro to copy it to your clipboard.
generate.title: Generate Document
generate.setupDescription: Choose a dossier, pick a template, then either generate immediately or review the draft before saving.
generate.reviewDescription: Review the resolved draft, adjust wording or formatting, then save the final file to the dossier.
generate.mode.directDescription: Resolve tags and write the final file to the dossier immediately.
generate.mode.reviewDescription: Build a draft first, adjust it manually in-app, then save the final file.
generate.tagsDescription: Review and adjust the tag values extracted from the template, then build the draft.
generate.tags.primaryContactHint: Select a contact to pre-fill {{contact.*}} tags. You can then edit each value manually.
generate.tags.roleContactHint: Select a contact for this role, or edit values manually.
generate.tags.keyDatesHint: Select a key date from the dossier, or type a date (DD/MM/YYYY). Formatted variants (.formatted, .long, .short) are derived automatically.
generate.tags.keyRefsHint: Select a key reference from the dossier, or enter a value manually.
generate.tags.otherTagsHint: Dossier, entity or system tags — edit if needed.
generate.tags.emptyPlaceholder: Leave empty to keep a placeholder
generate.docxPreviewUnavailable: Preview unavailable — the final document will be generated from the Word source file.
generate.reviewNotice: Edits in this review step affect only this generated document, not the source template.
generate.resolvedTagsHint: Click a value to insert it at the cursor.
generate.contactMapping.title: Contact mapping
generate.contactMapping.hint: Choose the primary contact for generic tags, then assign a contact to each role-based tag if needed.
generate.contactMapping.roleOverridesHin: This template also uses role-based tags — assign a contact to each role below.
generate.previewError: Unable to prepare the generated draft.
generate.saveError: Unable to save the generated document.
generate.errorDocxSourceMissing: Word source file not found. Re-import the .docx source in the template editor.
generate.unresolvedTagHint: Missing data is replaced with a visible placeholder so the document can still be reviewed and completed manually.
generate.docxSaveDescription: Choose the filename and save location for the generated Word document.
ai_settings.section_summary: Manage the AI assistant used for smart features.
ai_settings.none_info: AI features will be disabled. You can enable them at any time by selecting a mode.
ai_settings.cloud_managed_info: This mode sends your commands and contact data to an external AI provider. Make sure this complies with your professional confidentiality obligations.
ai_settings.claude_code_info: Claude Cowork uses your local Claude Code installation. No additional configuration required.
ai_settings.remote_warning: This mode sends your commands and contact data to an external AI provider. Make sure this complies with your professional confidentiality obligations.
ai_settings.pii_description: Pseudonymizes personal data before sending to the remote provider.
ai_settings.claude_code_warning_body: Claude Cowork is intended for experimental use or less regulated contexts. Commands and context data will be processed by Claude. Use with caution in corporate environments with controlled AI policies.
ai_settings.privacy_warning_body: Using this AI mode will transmit your commands and contact data to an external AI provider. This may violate professional confidentiality obligations. Are you sure you want to enable this mode?
ai_settings.connection_error_method_not_: This remote provider does not support this connection-check operation. Verify the API URL and try again.
ai_settings.project_ref_required_error: This reference is required for Infomaniak.
ai_settings.remote_flow_hint: Flow: choose a provider, add your API key, verify the connection, then save. For Infomaniak, project/workspace reference is required.
ai_settings.onboarding_title: Set up your local AI model
ai_settings.onboarding_step_2: Pull a model: ollama pull llama3
ai.panel.configure_message: Configure an AI mode in Settings to use this feature.
ai.panel.cloud_mode_message: You're using {mode} — run your CLI tool directly to interact with Ordicab.
ai.panel.placeholder: Ask the AI assistant... (e.g. 'Find Thomas Renard', 'Generate the NDA')
ai.panel.command_hints: Try: 'Find contacts', 'Select a template', or 'Generate a document'.
ai.page.empty_state: Send a command to get started.
ai.page.welcome_subtitle: Describe what you want to do in natural language.
ai.page.debug_copy_title: Copy the request sent to the LLM and its raw response
updater.ready_body: Restart now to apply the update?
